1. Install RabbitMQ Server on Ubuntu
echo "deb http://www.rabbitmq.com/debian/ testing main"  | sudo tee  /etc/apt/sources.list.d/rabbitmq.list > /dev/null
sudo wget http://www.rabbitmq.com/rabbitmq-signing-key-public.asc
sudo apt-key add rabbitmq-signing-key-public.asc
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install rabbitmq-server -y
sudo service rabbitmq-server start
sudo rabbitmq-plugins enable rabbitmq_management
sudo service rabbitmq-server restart

2. Install Python binding for RabbitMQ
#sudo apt-get install python-pip git-core
#sudo pip install pika==0.9.13

3. Add user and allow the user to connect remotely
3.1 Change the password for guest
#sudo rabbitmqctl change_password guest guest

3.2 Allow guest to connect remotely
A complete rabbitmq.config () which does this would look like:

[{rabbit, [{loopback_users, []}]}].

#sudo service rabbitmq-server restart
